Relevant only for usage-based billing orgs. Step 8: Verify and monitor Spot-check access : Check a few members from each group to confirm they see the right features. Spot-check access : Check a few members from each group to confirm they see the right features. Test the restricted state : Log in as (or ask) a member who should not have a feature like Cowork. They should see it greyed out with the message "Contact your admin to request access to this feature." Test the restricted state : Log in as (or ask) a member who should not have a feature like Cowork. They should see it greyed out with the message "Contact your admin to request access to this feature." Test the granted state : Confirm a member who should have the feature sees it working normally. Test the granted state : Confirm a member who should have the feature sees it working normally. Check edge cases : Test members in multiple groups, members with no group, and new members joining via SSO. Check edge cases : Test members in multiple groups, members with no group, and new members joining via SSO. Permission changes take up to five minutes to fully sync across the platform. Members may need to refresh their browser to see updated access. Using SCIM with role-based capabilities SCIM connects to your role-based capabilities through two mechanisms that work together. IdP group-to-role mapping This controls which built-in role a member gets when they're provisioned. Map your IdP groups to “Custom roles” so that new members' access is automatically governed by custom role capabilities. Navigate to Organization settings > Organization and access . Navigate to Organization settings > Organization and access . In the role mappings table, map your IdP groups to “Custom roles.” In the role mappings table, map your IdP groups to “Custom roles.” Group sync This pulls your IdP groups into Claude so they can be assigned to custom roles. Navigate to Organization settings > Groups Navigate to Organization settings > Groups Click “Check for updates” in the SCIM sync section. Click “Check for updates” in the SCIM sync section.
